workLog.task: Issue #1912 sub-failure 2: get_unaddressed_comments.py emitted a bare custom dict via json.dumps instead of the standard {Success, Data, Error, Metadata} skill output envelope used by get_pr_context.py, and it had no --output-format flag.
workLog.outcome: Read get_pr_context.py and github_core/output.py for the canonical pattern. Added add_output_format_arg to the parser (--output-format {json,human,auto}; auto detects TTY via sys.stdout.isatty()), resolved the format with get_output_format, and routed the result through write_skill_output so json emits the standard envelope and human prints a one-line summary. Removed the now-unused json import. Regenerated the src/copilot-cli mirror with build/scripts/build_all.py. Bumped plugin manifests: project-toolkit (.claude) 0.4.26 -> 0.4.27 and the Copilot mirror 0.4.37 -> 0.4.38. Updated existing main() tests to read output[Data][TotalCount]/[Comments] and added a TestOutputFormat class covering json (standard envelope), human (text summary, not JSON), auto-as-json (stdout redirected), and auto-as-human (stdout patched to a TTY).
workLog.evidence: uv run python -m pytest tests/test_get_unaddressed_comments.py -> 34 passed; tests/test_get_pr_context.py -> 22 passed; uv run python build/scripts/build_all.py --check -> no drift; uv run python build/scripts/validate_plugin_version_bump.py -> plugin-version-bump: OK; security scan -> No vulnerabilities found

nextSteps: Push feat/1912-output-format; orchestrator opens PR with Fixes #1912
Other github scripts that still emit bare dicts can adopt the same envelope in follow-up PRs
